hard_fail_conditions: importing SU(3), SU(2), U(1), or SU(3)xSU(2)xU(1) as a PREMISE (they may appear only as a discovered/labelled endpoint, in comparison text, or as a semantic label attached AFTER a derivation node)
importing quark, lepton, Higgs, gauge-boson, or any named particle as a premise
importing a named gauge equation (Yang-Mills action, Dirac equation, Higgs potential) as a premise
starting from a gauge group instead of from readout-preserving automorphisms Aut(F,O)

claiming a gauge group, representation set, generation count, mixing angle, or coupling constant is derived from the UNRESTRICTED root (finite/minimal-architecture closures ARE allowed and must say 'exact within the declared architecture', never 'derived from the root' unqualified)
reading AP20's self-interaction ratio or AP10's beta slopes as a root-derivation of the Standard Model
any claim that end-to-end root-derived Standard Model is > 0%

claiming this folder has a single end-to-end Standard Model verifier (it does not, by design); it DOES have node-level verifiers + Coq witnesses (run_tests.py), and that is a real, checkable, PASS/Closed status per node -- do not deny their existence and do not conflate node-level PASS with end-to-end closure
mixing the derivation chain and the empirical-discovery chain
calling a local Hessian / curvature at a minimum a PHYSICAL POLE MASS without a spectral-transfer certificate
calling a calibrated decoder (e.g. electroweak_decoder_v0_3) a PREDICTION rather than a calibration-consistency check

citing the SUPERSEDED exponential order-closure ansatz (Z_j=1+zeta e^{kappa r}) as a canonical or active result; the canonical order-vacuum result is intertwiner_order_vacuum_v1_13 (Fock factor (1+lambda r)^d, Z_j(0)=1)
declaring the order criterion Pi0>alpha PROVEN/FORCED before the primitive costs (Delta_j, alpha, beta) are derived from the root grammar -- it is currently a conditional criterion only
note: The Standard Model is a domain-translation to be DISCOVERED, never a root. The gauge group is a semantic label added only after group identification from data (the discovery chain).
prohibited_active_premise_tokens_scope: The token list below is a PREMISE-POSITION rule only: forbidden as an input assumption feeding a derivation. The SAME tokens are ALLOWED as: (a) a discovered/labelled endpoint after a derivation (e.g. 'the recovered algebra IS su(3)+su(2)+u(1)'), (b) a comparison to known physics for honesty/calibration, (c) a semantic label attached AFTER a blind search (e.g. v1.6's matter skeleton labels Q,u^c,d^c,L,e^c only after the search).
